change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value:
Form Local Disaster Management Committees (LDMC) at VDC level and train them on the inclusive and flexible local Disaster Risk Management Planning (LDRMP) process
	Carry out LDRMP process at VDC level and produce the LDRMP
	Train VDC board on mainstreaming DRR in their local planning process, linking LDRMP to VDC annual development plan
	Support Citizen Awareness Center (CAC) to facilitate community dialogue on DRR, empowerment, and governance and document evidence and lessons learned
	Train CSOs on DRR mainstreaming and advocacy for enhancing accountability of local authorities and service providers on DRR and support to participate in VDC regular planning and reconstruction process
	Support LDMC to implement small scale mitigation measures identified in LDRMP
	Support CSOs to organize multi-stakeholders dialogue at VDC level on DRR and reconstruction process
	Organize workshops and trainings at district level to orient on inclusive DRR and resilience approaches
	Facilitate and support DDRCs to develop tools and SOPs for coordination and information management for effective disaster response and implement SOP
	Provide training to members of the sectoral planning committees of DDC to mainstream DRR into district development planning process
	Build capacity of district level government agencies to monitor the implementation of inclusive LDRMP, DDP, and DPRP aligned with the local self-governance act (LSGA 1999) and minimum conditions performance measures (MCPM)
	Support DDRC to review and update the inclusive district preparedness and response plan (DPRPs) based on Vulnerability and Capacity Assessments (VCAs) from the VDCs.
	Workshop to review inclusive and flexible LDRMP model to agree on the process and key contents to roll out in the district and VDC level
	Conduct a study in stock taking the status of DRR mainstreaming in local development and recovery and reconstruction process and share its findings to concerned stakeholders Facilitate lobbying and interaction with CA members, parliament committees, senior government officials and political leaders in collaboration with media, civil society and peer organizations for the endorsement of the DRR policy/action plan based on Sendai framework for DRR
	Support concerned ministries (MOHA, NPC & MoFALD) to review / develop national DRM Policy /guidelines
	Provide inputs to Ministry of Federal Affairs and Local Development (MOFALD) on the Local Disaster Risk Management Planning (LDRMP) guidelines, based on learning from the four districts and the application of the inclusive and flexible LDRMP model
	Organize district and national sharing workshops on good practices among the key DRR stakeholders
	Train and support communities and market actors to construct and sustain “safe and accessible” toilets and develop Open Defecation Free strategies and WASH contingency plans
	Implement  inclusive hygiene promotion campaigns to restore and improve hygiene behaviors interrupted by the earthquakes and to build resilience in communities through behavior change
	Put in place risk-sensitive water supply systems for communities that have earthquake impacted water access and strengthen water supply system functionality by facilitating the re-forming of inclusive Water Supply Users Committees (WSUCs), building their capacity
	Support for restocking of small livestock and repairing relevant infrastructure in 2 VDCs
	Support most-vulnerable HHs with essential seeds, seeds storage facilities and irrigation channel repair
	Support capacity building/skill development trainings and materials based on community needs

en.full: Nepal Earthquake Response Programme- Disaster Risk Reduction in recovery and reconstruction for most affected population in Nepal

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
